institution.name: Boston Medical Center
aliases: Boston University Chobanian & Avedisian School of Medicine — International Student Elective Program (ISEP)

searchTermsTried: Boston University School of Medicine visiting medical student VSLO international Boston Medical Center site:bu.edu
Boston Medical Center visiting medical student

candidateFindings.shortQuote: Be in his/her final year of medical school; Be in good academic standing at his/her home institution; Be fluent in the English language; Not be enrolled in a Caribbean medical school; and Not have earned an MD degree.
Students should request a B-1/B-2 visitor visa at their home embassy or consulate for their visit; if a student requires a letter of invitation from BUCASM for this purpose, he/she may request it.

negativeEvidence.strongNegativeEvidence: Two explicit verbatim exclusions found: Caribbean medical school students AND MD-degree-holders are not eligible for ISEP. This is the first P101 packet with both exclusions captured verbatim — important to prevent IMG-grad overclaim on BMC.
negativeEvidence.weakNegativeEvidence: Application process specifics not captured verbatim on the hub; the application instructions live on a sub-page.

nextAction: For public-safe activation: (a) INSTITUTION_SPECIFIC — BU CASM ISEP at BMC; (b) international final-year-MS only; (c) Caribbean-school + MD-grad explicit exclusions; (d) tuition $3,000 per 4-wk, max 2 electives; (e) B-1/B-2 visa with BUCASM invitation letter available.
plainEnglishSummary: Boston Medical Center accepts international visiting medical students through BU's International Student Elective Program. Final-year medical students only. Caribbean schools are explicitly excluded; MD graduates (observers) are explicitly excluded. Tuition is $3,000 per 4-week elective, max two electives. Students apply for a B-1/B-2 visa; BUCASM provides an invitation letter.
driftCheck: This advanced national USCE discovery because Massachusetts gained its first P101 packet — a confirmed international-eligible IMG-relevant lane at BMC with the strongest verbatim audience-exclusion language found in P101 to date (both Caribbean and MD-grad explicit).
